title: fraxinus micrantha
intro: Fraxinus micrantha, also known as the small-leaved ash or Manchurian ash, is a deciduous tree species native to East Asia. The medicinal properties of Fraxinus micrantha lie in its bark, which contains salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, and is used to treat pain, reduce inflammation, and lower fever. The health benefits of this plant include relief from arthritis, rheumatism, and other inflammatory conditions, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. Fraxinus micrantha can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to treat various health issues and promote overall well-being.
